meta.title: W202 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every Mercedes C-Class W202 engine — M111, M104, M112, M113, OM604, OM605, OM611. Real UK costs, honest reliability data, and AMG variants explained. Get free quotes from 25+ vetted Mercedes specialists today.

mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes C-Class W202 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned W202 engine typically costs £1,200–£7,000 supply only, depending on engine — the volume M111 four-cylinder petrol sits at £1,200–£3,500, while the M113 V8 in the C43 AMG is the most expensive at £4,000–£7,000. Fitting adds £650–£1,300 depending on engine complexity and specialist rates. Use our calculator for a personalised figure.

mainEntity.name: Which Mercedes C-Class W202 engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The OM605 five-cylinder diesel (C250 Turbodiesel, 150 PS) is widely considered the most bulletproof engine in the W202 range — legendary Mercedes over-engineering with a straightforward mechanical fuel injection system. The M111 four-cylinder petrol (C180, C200) is also highly reliable when the biodegradable wiring harness has been replaced.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes C-Class W202?

acceptedAnswer.text: Biodegradable wiring harness failure on pre-1997 petrol cars, head gasket issues on the M104 straight-six, glow plug and injector wear on the OM60x diesels, and steering gear play on early examples. Cooling system neglect is a common cause of head gasket failure across all engines.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes C-Class W202 reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Overall reliability is good for its era, but the W202 has two significant weak points: the biodegradable wiring harness (pre-1997) and the M104 straight-six head gasket. Once these are addressed, the W202 is a robust, well-engineered car. The OM605 diesel is particularly durable and can exceed 300,000 miles with proper maintenance.

mainEntity.name: Which Mercedes C-Class W202 engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: The OM604 diesel (C200/C220 Diesel) is underpowered and suffers from indirect-injection drawbacks — we'd recommend the OM605 instead. Also avoid any pre-1997 petrol car without documented wiring harness replacement — chasing electrical gremlins can be a costly nightmare.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my W202 engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Persistent misfires or rough idle on petrol engines (wiring harness or coil issues), poor cold starting or excessive smoke on diesels (glow plug/injector wear), oil foam in the coolant expansion tank (M104 head gasket), or a loud tapping from the top end (camshaft or hydraulic tappet wear) are the key warning signs. Use our diagnostic calculator for a personalised assessment.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes C-Class W202 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on the car's value. Against a well-kept £4,000+ C280, a £2,000–£4,000 recon is 50–100% of the car's value — a serious decision. Against a tired £1,500 C180, a £1,200–£2,500 recon is 80–167% — walk away. Our 70% Mercedes-Benz threshold is the line. Note that C36 and C43 AMG values are significantly higher and may justify engine replacement where a standard C-Class would not.

mainEntity.name: How long do Mercedes C-Class W202 engines last?

acceptedAnswer.text: The M111 four-cylinder petrol can exceed 200,000 miles with regular servicing and prompt wiring harness replacement. The M104 straight-six is similarly durable but requires head gasket attention around 80,000–130,000 miles. The OM605 five-cylinder diesel is the endurance champion — 300,000+ miles is achievable with proper maintenance.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between the W202, W203, and W204 C-Class?

acceptedAnswer.text: The W202 (1993–2000) is the first-generation C-Class — the successor to the 190E. The W203 (2000–2007) is the second generation with round headlights and more modern electronics. The W204 (2007–2014) is the third generation with angular styling. Engines are generally not interchangeable between generations. See our W203 and W204 pages for those generations' specifics.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes C-Class W202 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate (under the bonnet or on the driver's door pillar) and on the engine block itself — the M111 petrol engines are visible from above, while the OM605 diesel requires slight disassembly to access the block stamping. Tell us your variant (C180, C200, C220, C230, C240, C280, C36 AMG, C43 AMG, C200 Diesel, C220 Diesel, C250 Diesel, C250 Turbodiesel, C220 CDI) and we'll identify it — 25+ vetted Mercedes specialists will confirm it for free.

hero.h1: Mercedes C-Class W202 Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the first-generation C-Class — from the M111 four-cylinder petrol to the M104 straight-six and the OM605 five-cylinder diesel that defined Mercedes' compact executive class. Real UK costs, honest reliability data, and the engine-harness issues that plague early cars. Backed by real UK enquiries in 2025 [PATTERN DATA].

engineDatabase.h2: Mercedes C-Class W202 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the W202 saloon and S202 estate — including the full M111 petrol range, the M104 straight-six, and the OM60x diesel family.

overview.intro: The W202 is the first-generation Mercedes-Benz C-Class, produced from 1992 to 2000 as the successor to the 190E (W201). It established the C-Class as Mercedes' compact executive offering, available as a four-door saloon and, from 1995, a five-door estate (S202). The engine range spanned M111 four-cylinder petrol units from 1.8 to 2.3 litres, M104 and M112 six-cylinder petrols, the M113 V8 in AMG guise, plus OM604, OM605 and OM611 diesel units. A mild facelift in 1997 brought revised styling and mechanical updates.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: Mercedes W202 (first generation) • Predecessor: Mercedes-Benz 190E (W201) • Successor: Mercedes-Benz C-Class (W203) • Sibling body styles in this generation: S202 estate (1995–2001).
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: The W202 C-Class generated UK engine enquiries in 2025 [PATTERN DATA], with the M111 petrol fours and OM605 diesel five-cylinders remaining the most commonly discussed among specialists.

items.quote: Thin-Data Fallback mode — one paragraph: The W202's most dependable engine is the M104 2.8-litre straight-six (C280) — the 193 PS six-cylinder is widely considered the pick of the range for its smooth power delivery and proven durability, though early cars (pre-1997) suffer from biodegradable wiring harness failure. The M111 four-cylinder range is generally robust — the 1.8L C180 and 2.0L C200 are simple, reliable units with minimal electronic complexity. The OM605 five-cylinder diesel (C250 Turbodiesel) is the best diesel option, offering 150 PS with the legendary Mercedes over-engineered build quality. The OM611 CDI (C220 CDI) introduced common-rail technology in 1999 and is a significant step forward in refinement and economy. Avoid early OM604 diesels — they are underpowered and suffer from indirect-injection drawbacks, and avoid any pre-1997 car without documented wiring harness replacement, as the biodegradable looms are a known failure point.

rows.ownershipVerdict: Best Value — simple, reliable, cheap to run
Solid Choice — more power, same reliability
Upgrade Pick — 2.3L torque without Kompressor complexity
Fun Choice — supercharged but more to maintain
Most Refined — smooth six, but harness watch
Modern Six — V6 refinement, post-harness issues
Collector's Item — rare, costly to maintain
V8 Icon — the first V8 C-Class, specialist only

rows.commonMajorFailure: Glow plugs, injector seals, fuel system
Glow plugs, injector seals, turbo (on TD)

economicsRule.title: The W202 rule
economicsRule.text: A £2,000–£4,000 recon on a sub-£2,500 W202 sits at 80–160% of the car's value — at that point, scrap value usually wins. Against a well-kept £4,000+ example, the same recon drops to 50–100% — still a serious decision, but one that can make sense if the car is otherwise sound. Value the car first, then the engine. Note that C36 and C43 AMG values are significantly higher and may justify engine replacement where a standard C-Class would not.

keyTakeaways.question: Which W202 Costs Least To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: C180 (M111.920) — simplest engine, cheapest parts, no turbo or supercharger complexity.

keyTakeaways.question: Which W202 Costs Most To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: C43 AMG (M113.944) — V8 parts premium, specialist labour required.

keyTakeaways.question: Which W202 Is Best Value?
keyTakeaways.answer: C250 Turbodiesel (OM605.912) — 150 PS, bulletproof five-cylinder, excellent economy.

problems.title: M111 Petrol Wiring Harness Degradation
problems.affectedModels: W202 C180, C200, C220, C230 (all M111 petrol variants, pre-1997 particularly).
problems.rootCause: Early W202s used biodegradable wiring insulation that degrades with age and heat, causing short circuits, misfires, and erratic engine behaviour. The upper engine harness is the primary culprit, but lower harnesses can also fail.
problems.recommendation: If your W202 has no documented wiring harness replacement, budget for it immediately — £400–£700 is cheap insurance against the £1,000+ bill for chasing intermittent electrical faults. This is the single most important preventative job on any early W202.

cta.label: Compare M111 engine prices for your W202 C-Class →

problems.title: M104 Straight-Six Head Gasket Failure
problems.rootCause: The M104's head gasket can fail, allowing oil and coolant to mix — visible as oil foam in the coolant expansion tank. This is often caused by cooling system neglect or the biodegradable wiring harness causing the engine to run incorrectly.

tieredCosts.work: Full engine rebuild if warped or cracked

problems.recommendation: Check the coolant expansion tank regularly for oil foam — if spotted, have the head gasket inspected immediately. A £700–£1,200 repair is far cheaper than the £2,000+ replacement that follows if the engine overheats and warps.

cta.label: Compare M104 engine prices for your W202 C280 →

problems.title: OM605/OM604 Diesel Glow Plug & Injector Issues
problems.affectedModels: W202 C200 Diesel, C220 Diesel, C250 Diesel, C250 Turbodiesel (all OM60x diesel variants).
problems.rootCause: The OM60x series uses indirect injection with pre-combustion chambers. Glow plugs wear out over time, causing poor cold starting and increased smoke. Injector seals can leak, causing fuel contamination of the engine oil.

tieredCosts.work: Replace glow plugs and inspect injectors
Full injector replacement and fuel system flush

problems.recommendation: If your W202 diesel is hard to start when cold, have the glow plugs tested immediately — £200–£400 is cheap insurance against the £1,000+ bill that follows if failed glow plugs cause injector damage or hydraulic lock.

cta.label: Compare OM605 engine prices for your W202 diesel →

problems.title: Early W202 Steering Play & Suspension Wear
problems.rootCause: Early W202s suffer from premature wear of the steering gear and steering rods, leading to vague steering and play in the wheel. This is not an engine failure but is one of the most common W202 enquiries.

tieredCosts.work: Replace steering rods and track rod ends

problems.recommendation: Have the steering and suspension checked at every service — £300–£500 in preventative work is a fraction of the £800+ cost if worn components cause MOT failure or accident damage.

cta.label: Compare W202 steering and suspension repair quotes →
